title: Fix typings/i10n.d.ts syntax error in plugins with no Fluent strings
description:
zotero-plugin-scaffold's build step generates `typings/i10n.d.ts` containing a FluentMessageId union of all known Fluent message IDs. When a plugin has no Fluent strings, the union is empty, producing invalid TypeScript:

  export type FluentMessageId =
  ;

This causes \`tsc --noEmit\` (the second half of \`npm run build\`) to fail with:

  typings/i10n.d.ts(6,1): error TS1110: Type expected.

The XPI itself is unaffected (esbuild ignores .d.ts files), but \`npm run build\` exits non-zero, which is noisy and can break CI.

Confirmed in: packages/zotero-outline-page-labels (after revert to scaffold 0.8.2). Worth checking whether packages/zotero-export-notes is also affected (it has no Fluent strings either; previous build was clean only because the menu.ts error masked it).

Two viable fixes:
- Add a placeholder Fluent file with at least one message id, OR
- Exclude typings/i10n.d.ts in tsconfig.json

The @ts-nocheck directive at the top of the generated file does NOT suppress syntactic errors -- only semantic ones.

title: Upgrade plugin dependencies in both packages
description:
Both plugins still on pre-release toolkit/scaffold/types after the Zotero 9 compat work:

- zotero-plugin-toolkit ^5.1.0-beta.13 -> latest stable 5.1.x
- zotero-plugin-scaffold ^0.8.2 -> 0.8.6+
- zotero-types ^4.1.0-beta.4 -> 4.1.x

Blocked by the menu.ts migration (separate issue) since toolkit 5.1.2 removed ztoolkit.Menu.

Packages:
- packages/zotero-export-notes/package.json
- packages/zotero-outline-page-labels/package.json (does not use ztoolkit.Menu, could be upgraded independently as a smaller test)

title: Smoke-test HTTP API clients against Zotero 9
description:
The original Zotero 9 compat plan included verifying the non-plugin HTTP API clients still work under 9.0.2. Was deferred when bandwidth ran out.

To exercise:
- packages/zotero-cli (Python): \`uv run zotero-collection --list\`, \`uv run zotero-get-annots <KEY> --org\`. Verify against /api/users/0/items, /api/users/0/items/{key}/children, /api/users/0/collections.
- packages/zotero-elisp: M-x zotero-list-libraries, then exercise org-zotero-client annotation export on one item.
- packages/zotero-upload-url: \`uv run zotero-collection --list\` (uses native API), \`uv run zotero-save "https://example.com"\` (Zotero Connector path).

Likely-affected surface: Zotero 9 added new item types and fields after a 15-year hiatus. Parsing code in packages/zotero-cli/src/zotero_cli/api.py (ZoteroLocalAPI) and packages/zotero-elisp/zotero-api.el should be checked for assumptions that break on new types.

title: Migrate menu.ts from ztoolkit.Menu to Zotero.MenuManager
description:
zotero-plugin-toolkit 5.1.x removed the `Menu` manager (`ZoteroToolkit.Menu` no longer exists). `packages/zotero-export-notes/src/modules/menu.ts:24` calls `ztoolkit.Menu.register("item", { ... })` which fails to typecheck and breaks the right-click "Export Annotations" menu at runtime when toolkit is upgraded.

During the Zotero 9 compat work (2026-05-04), `npm update --save` was reverted because of this. Both plugins are still pinned at `zotero-plugin-toolkit ^5.1.0-beta.13` to keep the Menu API. To unblock dep upgrades, port `MenuFactory.registerItemContextMenu()` to use Zotero 7+'s native `Zotero.MenuManager.registerMenu()` API directly (the toolkit removed its wrapper because Zotero now ships a native one).

Once migrated, follow up by running `npm update --save` in both plugin packages and verifying the menu still works.

Affected file:
- packages/zotero-export-notes/src/modules/menu.ts (MenuFactory class)

Test: rebuild the plugin, install, right-click an item with annotations in Zotero 9 -> confirm "Export Annotations" submenu appears with Markdown and Org subitems.

title: Publish v1.0.1 GitHub releases for both plugins
description:
After the Zotero 9 compat work, both plugin packages were bumped to v1.0.1 and rebuilt locally. The .scaffold/build/update.json files reference \`https://github.com/.../releases/download/v1.0.1/<plugin>.xpi\` -- that release does not exist yet on GitHub.

For users with auto-update via update_url to work, publish v1.0.1 GitHub releases:
- pricemat/zotero-export-notes: tag v1.0.1, attach zotero-export-org-notes.xpi + update.json
- pricemat/mwp-zotero-tools: tag v1.0.1 (or per-plugin tags) for zotero-outline-page-labels.xpi + update.json

For zotero-outline-page-labels specifically, this is its first public release -- decide on the canonical update_url scheme before publishing.
